title: Multi-channel EEG recordings during a sustained-attention driving task
description: This dataset comprises multi-channel EEG recordings from 27 healthy adults performing a sustained-attention driving task in a virtual-reality simulator. The raw dataset contains 62 EEG sessions (~82 hours total) acquired at 500 Hz using a 32-channel Quik-Cap system, with event markers for lane-departure trials and steering responses. The data includes 27,192 lane-deviation events designed to assess fatigue and drowsiness through reaction time measurements, and is provided in BIDS-EEG format with nominal 10-20 electrode positions.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a Compumedics Neuroscan Scan SynAmps2 Express amplifier with a 32-channel Quik-Cap (Ag/AgCl electrodes) at 500 Hz sampling rate and 16-bit quantization. Participants performed a virtual-reality driving task on a six-degree-of-freedom Stewart motion platform with a real Ford Probe car frame, maintaining lane position on a simulated night-time highway while responding to randomly induced left/right lane-departure events. Electrode impedance was maintained below 5 kOhm. A 33rd channel recorded simulated vehicle lateral position (0-255 quantization) synchronized with EEG acquisition.
